---
title: "CB3 Economic Security and Food Maps"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: python3
---

## Economic Security and Food Maps

This notebook renders four Economic Security and Food topic maps from the
map-ready clean file:

`data/clean/economic-food.csv`

Each output map has exactly one primary bubble layer. Supporting metrics stay
in the clean file and appear in the bubble tooltip, so the maps do not lose
context while avoiding multiple bubble layers.

Emergency food provider coordinates are loaded from:

`data/clean/emergency_food_providers.csv`

The provider locations are added to the food-insecurity map as a selectable
categorical point layer using `src.map_utils.add_categorical_point_layer()`.

The notebook does not create or use `map_files/`.

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import sys

import geopandas as gpd
import pandas as pd
import yaml
from shapely import wkt

PROJECT_DIR = Path.cwd()
while not (PROJECT_DIR / "src").exists() and PROJECT_DIR != PROJECT_DIR.parent:
    PROJECT_DIR = PROJECT_DIR.parent

if str(PROJECT_DIR) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(PROJECT_DIR))

from src.map_utils import (
    add_categorical_point_layer,
    build_metric_bubble_map,
    find_project_dir,
    format_metric_value,
)

PROJECT_DIR = find_project_dir()
CLEAN_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "economic-food.csv"
PROVIDER_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "emergency_food_providers.csv"
METADATA_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "docs" / "metric_metadata.yml"
OUTPUT_DIR = PROJECT_DIR / "maps" / "economic-food"
OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

PRIMARY_DOMAIN = "economic-security-food"
CONTEXT_DOMAIN = "economic-security-food-context"
EXCLUDED_DEMOGRAPHIC_METRICS = {"senior_poverty_rate"}
```

```{python}
def to_map_spec(meta, include_filename=False):
    """Convert metric_metadata.yml entries into the spec expected by map_utils."""
    spec = {
        "dimension": meta["short_label"],
        "label": meta["label"],
        "unit": meta["unit"],
    }

    if "palette" in meta:
        spec["palette"] = meta["palette"]

    if include_filename:
        spec["filename"] = meta["filename"]

    for optional_key in ("positive_values_only", "zero_color", "zero_label"):
        if optional_key in meta:
            spec[optional_key] = meta[optional_key]

    return spec


with open(METADATA_PATH, encoding="utf-8") as f:
    all_metrics = yaml.safe_load(f) or {}

provider_point_spec = all_metrics.get(
    "emergency_food_provider_locations",
    {},
)
provider_category_colors = provider_point_spec.get(
    "category_colors",
    {
        "Emergency food provider": "#7a0177",
    },
)

primary_specs = {
    variable: to_map_spec(meta, include_filename=True)
    for variable, meta in all_metrics.items()
    if meta.get("domain") == PRIMARY_DOMAIN
}

context_specs = {
    variable: to_map_spec(meta, include_filename=True)
    for variable, meta in all_metrics.items()
    if meta.get("domain") == CONTEXT_DOMAIN
}

demographic_specs = {
    variable: to_map_spec(meta)
    for variable, meta in all_metrics.items()
    if meta.get("domain") == "demographics"
    and variable not in EXCLUDED_DEMOGRAPHIC_METRICS
}

if not primary_specs:
    raise ValueError(f"No primary map metrics found with domain: {PRIMARY_DOMAIN}")

if not demographic_specs:
    raise ValueError("No demographic metrics found in metric_metadata.yml.")

print("Primary Economic/Food map metrics:")
for variable, spec in primary_specs.items():
    print(f"  {variable} -> {spec['filename']}")

print("\nContext metrics available for tooltips:")
for variable in context_specs:
    print(f"  {variable}")

print("\nDemographic backdrop metrics:")
for variable in demographic_specs:
    print(f"  {variable}")
```

## Four map topics

```{python}
MAP_TOPICS = {
    "safety_net_reliance": {
        "primary_metric": "safety_net_enrollment_per_capita",
        "title": "CB3 Economic Security & Food: Safety-net reliance",
        "subtitle": (
            "Bubbles show safety-net enrollment per capita. "
            "Tooltip also shows poverty and safety-net program counts where available. "
            "Use the layer control to pick a demographic background."
        ),
        "context_metrics": [
            "poverty_rate_pct",
            "poverty_count",
            "poverty_universe_population",
            "medicaid_enrollment",
            "snap_enrollment",
            "cash_assistance_enrollment",
            "safety_net_enrollment",
        ],
    },
    "labor_market_conditions": {
        "primary_metric": "unemployment_rate_pct",
        "title": "CB3 Economic Security & Food: Labor-market conditions",
        "subtitle": (
            "Bubbles show unemployment rate. "
            "Tooltip also shows labor-force size, unemployed population, median earnings, "
            "and household income where available. Use the layer control to pick a demographic background."
        ),
        "context_metrics": [
            "civilian_labor_force",
            "unemployed_population",
            "median_earnings_all_workers",
            "median_household_income",
        ],
    },
    "food_insecurity": {
        "primary_metric": "food_insecurity_rate_pct",
        "title": "CB3 Economic Security & Food: Food insecurity",
        "subtitle": (
            "Bubbles show tract-level food insecurity rate. "
            "Tooltip also shows NTA-level food supply gap rank, score, food-insecure share, "
            "and supply gap pounds where available. Emergency food provider locations are "
            "available as a selectable point overlay."
        ),
        "context_metrics": [
            "food_supply_gap_rank_nta",
            "food_supply_gap_score_nta",
            "food_supply_gap_food_insecure_pct_nta",
            "food_supply_gap_lbs_nta",
        ],
    },
    "utility_shutoff_risk": {
        "primary_metric": "utility_shutoff_risk_pct",
        "title": "CB3 Economic Security & Food: Utility shutoff risk",
        "subtitle": (
            "Bubbles show estimated utility shutoff risk. "
            "Use the layer control to pick a demographic background."
        ),
        "context_metrics": [],
    },
}
```

## Load prepared clean map file

```{python}
if not CLEAN_PATH.exists():
    raise FileNotFoundError(
        f"Could not find {CLEAN_PATH}. Run scripts/economic-food/build.py first."
    )

clean = pd.read_csv(CLEAN_PATH, dtype={"GEOID": "string"}, low_memory=False)

required_columns = {
    "GEOID",
    "tract_label",
    "nta_name",
    "tract_centroid_latitude",
    "tract_centroid_longitude",
    "geometry_wkt",
}
missing_required = sorted(required_columns - set(clean.columns))
if missing_required:
    raise ValueError(
        "economic-food.csv is not map-ready. Missing columns: "
        + ", ".join(missing_required)
        + ". Re-run the refactored scripts/economic-food/build.py."
    )

clean["GEOID"] = clean["GEOID"].astype(str).str.extract(r"(\d+)")[0].str.zfill(11)
clean["geometry"] = clean["geometry_wkt"].map(wkt.loads)

tracts = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    clean.drop(columns=["geometry_wkt"]),
    geometry="geometry",
    crs="EPSG:4326",
)

all_spec_columns = set(primary_specs) | set(context_specs) | set(demographic_specs)
for column in all_spec_columns:
    if column in tracts.columns:
        tracts[column] = pd.to_numeric(tracts[column], errors="coerce")

available_primary_specs = {
    metric: spec
    for metric, spec in primary_specs.items()
    if metric in tracts.columns and tracts[metric].notna().any()
}

available_context_specs = {
    metric: spec
    for metric, spec in context_specs.items()
    if metric in tracts.columns and tracts[metric].notna().any()
}

available_demographic_specs = {
    metric: spec
    for metric, spec in demographic_specs.items()
    if metric in tracts.columns and tracts[metric].notna().any()
}

assert len(tracts) == 31, f"Expected 31 CB3 tracts, got {len(tracts)}"
assert tracts["GEOID"].is_unique

missing_primary = [
    topic["primary_metric"]
    for topic in MAP_TOPICS.values()
    if topic["primary_metric"] not in available_primary_specs
]
if missing_primary:
    raise ValueError(
        "These four-map primary metrics are missing or all null in economic-food.csv/YAML: "
        + ", ".join(missing_primary)
    )

if not available_demographic_specs:
    raise ValueError(
        "No demographic backdrop columns are available in data/clean/economic-food.csv. "
        "Make sure build.py merged a baseline file or that economic-food.csv already contains demographics."
    )

print("Available primary map metrics:")
for metric, spec in available_primary_specs.items():
    print(f"  {metric}: {tracts[metric].notna().sum()} tracts -> {spec['filename']}")

print("\nAvailable context metrics:")
for metric, spec in available_context_specs.items():
    print(f"  {metric}: {tracts[metric].notna().sum()} tracts")

print("\nAvailable demographic backdrops:")
for metric in available_demographic_specs:
    print(f"  {metric}: {tracts[metric].notna().sum()} tracts")
```

## Load emergency food provider locations

```{python}
def load_provider_points(path):
    """Load map-ready provider coordinates created by economic-food/build.py."""
    if not path.exists():
        print(
            "Warning: emergency food provider layer unavailable because "
            "data/clean/emergency_food_providers.csv was not created. "
            "The separate raw provider-location source is missing."
        )
        return pd.DataFrame(
            columns=[
                "provider_name",
                "address",
                "provider_subtype",
                "provider_count",
                "provider_type",
                "location_level",
                "nta_name",
                "latitude",
                "longitude",
            ]
        )

    providers = pd.read_csv(path, low_memory=False)

    required = {
        "provider_type",
        "provider_count",
        "location_level",
        "latitude",
        "longitude",
    }
    missing = sorted(required - set(providers.columns))
    if missing:
        raise ValueError(
            "emergency_food_providers.csv is missing required columns: "
            + ", ".join(missing)
        )

    providers["latitude"] = pd.to_numeric(
        providers["latitude"],
        errors="coerce",
    )
    providers["longitude"] = pd.to_numeric(
        providers["longitude"],
        errors="coerce",
    )
    providers["provider_count"] = pd.to_numeric(
        providers["provider_count"],
        errors="coerce",
    ).fillna(1)

    if "provider_type" not in providers.columns:
        providers["provider_type"] = "Emergency food provider"

    providers["provider_type"] = (
        providers["provider_type"]
        .fillna("Emergency food provider")
        .astype(str)
        .str.strip()
    )
    providers.loc[
        providers["provider_type"].eq(""),
        "provider_type",
    ] = "Emergency food provider"

    if "provider_name" in providers.columns:
        providers["provider_name"] = (
            providers["provider_name"]
            .fillna("Emergency food provider")
            .astype(str)
            .str.strip()
        )

    if "address" in providers.columns:
        providers["address"] = providers["address"].fillna("").astype(str).str.strip()

    providers = providers.dropna(
        subset=["latitude", "longitude"]
    ).copy()

    if providers.empty:
        raise ValueError(
            "emergency_food_providers.csv contains no usable map markers."
        )

    print(
        "Emergency food provider points available: "
        f"{len(providers)}"
    )
    return providers


providers = load_provider_points(PROVIDER_PATH)

provider_types = sorted(
    providers["provider_type"].dropna().unique()
)

unregistered_provider_types = [
    provider_type
    for provider_type in provider_types
    if provider_type not in provider_category_colors
]
if unregistered_provider_types:
    raise KeyError(
        "Provider types missing from "
        "emergency_food_provider_locations.category_colors: "
        + ", ".join(unregistered_provider_types)
    )

provider_total = int(
    pd.to_numeric(
        providers["provider_count"],
        errors="coerce",
    ).fillna(1).sum()
)

if providers.empty:
    MAP_TOPICS["food_insecurity"]["subtitle"] = (
        "Bubbles show tract-level food insecurity rate. "
        "Tooltip also shows NTA-level food supply gap rank, score, "
        "food-insecure share, and supply gap pounds where available. "
        "Emergency food provider locations are not shown because the "
        "separate provider-location source is unavailable."
    )


def add_food_provider_layer(map_object):
    """Add provider markers before LayerControl is created by map_utils."""
    if providers.empty:
        return

    tooltip_fields = []
    tooltip_aliases = []

    for column, alias in [
        ("provider_name", "Provider"),
        ("provider_count", "Providers represented"),
        ("provider_subtype", "Provider subtype"),
        ("address", "Address"),
        ("nta_name", "NTA"),
        ("location_level", "Location level"),
    ]:
        if column in providers.columns:
            tooltip_fields.append(column)
            tooltip_aliases.append(alias)

    add_categorical_point_layer(
        map_object,
        providers,
        category_col="provider_type",
        category_colors=provider_category_colors,
        name=f"Emergency food providers (n={provider_total})",
        lat_col="latitude",
        lon_col="longitude",
        tooltip_fields=tooltip_fields,
        tooltip_aliases=tooltip_aliases,
        size=15,
        legend_bottom_offset=265,
    )
```

## Add formatted context columns for tooltips

```{python}
def add_context_display_column(frame, metric, spec):
    display_col = f"{metric}_display"
    frame[display_col] = frame[metric].map(
        lambda value: format_metric_value(value, spec["unit"])
    )
    return display_col


context_display_columns = {}
for metric, spec in {**available_context_specs, **available_primary_specs}.items():
    if metric in tracts.columns:
        context_display_columns[metric] = add_context_display_column(tracts, metric, spec)
```

## Render four maps

```{python}
rendered = {}

for topic_name, topic in MAP_TOPICS.items():
    primary_metric = topic["primary_metric"]
    spec = available_primary_specs[primary_metric]
    output_path = OUTPUT_DIR / spec["filename"]

    tooltip_fields = ["tract_label", "nta_name"]
    tooltip_aliases = ["Census tract", "NTA"]

    for context_metric in topic["context_metrics"]:
        if context_metric in context_display_columns:
            tooltip_fields.append(context_display_columns[context_metric])
            tooltip_aliases.append(
                available_context_specs.get(
                    context_metric,
                    available_primary_specs.get(context_metric),
                )["label"]
            )

    extra_layers = (
        add_food_provider_layer
        if topic_name == "food_insecurity" and not providers.empty
        else None
    )

    map_object = build_metric_bubble_map(
        tracts=tracts,
        metric_specs=available_primary_specs,
        demographic_specs=available_demographic_specs,
        metric=primary_metric,
        title=topic["title"],
        subtitle=topic["subtitle"],
        output_path=output_path,
        tooltip_fields=tooltip_fields,
        tooltip_aliases=tooltip_aliases,
        extra_layers=extra_layers,
    )

    rendered[topic_name] = {
        "metric": primary_metric,
        "map": map_object,
        "output_path": output_path,
    }

    print(f"Saved {topic_name}: {output_path.relative_to(PROJECT_DIR)}")

print(f"\nRendered {len(rendered)} Economic/Food topic maps.")
```

## Preview first rendered map

```{python}
first_topic = next(iter(rendered))
rendered[first_topic]["map"]
```

## Validation summary

```{python}
summary = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {
            "topic": topic_name,
            "primary_metric": details["metric"],
            "label": available_primary_specs[details["metric"]]["label"],
            "html_file": available_primary_specs[details["metric"]]["filename"],
            "tracts_with_data": int(tracts[details["metric"]].notna().sum()),
            "provider_markers": (
                len(providers)
                if topic_name == "food_insecurity"
                else 0
            ),
            "providers_represented": (
                provider_total
                if topic_name == "food_insecurity"
                else 0
            ),
            "output_exists": details["output_path"].exists(),
            "output_size_bytes": details["output_path"].stat().st_size,
        }
        for topic_name, details in rendered.items()
    ]
)

display(summary)

for details in rendered.values():
    path = details["output_path"]
    assert path.exists(), path
    assert path.stat().st_size > 10_000, path

print("Output files:")
for topic_name, details in rendered.items():
    print(f"  {topic_name}: {details['output_path'].relative_to(PROJECT_DIR)}")
```
